title: Ketan murder case: Siya, Chetan's video from cricket stadium goes viral amid murder probe
content: A viral video allegedly showing Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary at a cricket match has surfaced amid a murder investigation. Police believe their relationship predated Siya's engagement to Ketan Agarwal, with her family allegedly proceeding with the marriage despite knowing about the affair, citing financial reasons. Investigators are examining thousands of calls and frequent meetings between Siya and Chetan, alleging they planned Ketan's murder after multiple failed attempts.

title: 'Important to place address in context': Centre on Rajnath Singh's Op Sindoor deaths remark
content: The Defence Ministry has refuted claims that Defence Minister Rajnath Singh misled Parliament regarding casualties in Operation Sindoor. The ministry clarified that the minister's July 2025 address specifically countered a false narrative about Indian pilots being lost, not about soldiers' sacrifices. This statement follows the recent release of six soldiers' names killed during the May 2025 operation against Pakistan-backed terrorists.

title: Pregnant woman dies by suicide after husband demands DNA test for unborn child
content: A 23-year-old pregnant woman tragically died by suicide after her husband reportedly demanded a DNA test for their unborn child.  The incident, which occurred in Medak district, led to the arrest of the husband and his mother.  The victim's mother alleged continuous harassment, prompting police to register a case of cruelty and abetment of suicide under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Zohran Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: Telangana student found dead in London after attending birthday party
content: A 25-year-old Telangana student, S Srinath Reddy, pursuing studies in London, was found deceased on June 23. His father confirmed Srinath last spoke to the family on June 22, after which he reportedly attended a birthday party. His roommate allegedly discovered him dead the following morning, with initial reports suggesting suicide. The family is seeking government assistance to bring his body back home.

title: 'Lack of game awareness was visible': Ex-cricketer slams Iyer's captaincy calls
content: India suffered a 34-run defeat in the first T20I against Ireland, with former opener Aakash Chopra highlighting concerns over bowler Prasidh Krishna's expensive spell. Despite Krishna's wicket-taking ability, his high economy rate in T20Is, conceding 11.5 runs per over, has become a significant worry for the team.  Krishna will aim for a better performance in the next match as India seeks to level the series.

title: NY Mayor Zohran Mamdani files motion to ban delivery app Motoclick: What lawsuit says
content: New York City is seeking to ban food delivery platform Motoclick for allegedly paying workers as little as $3.67 per hour, a fraction of the mandated $22.13 minimum wage. City officials filed a motion for a preliminary injunction, aiming to halt the company's operations unless it complies with labor laws.

title: 'Wow': Irfan Pathan reacts to India's jaw-dropping defeat to Ireland
content: Ireland scripted history by defeating India for the first time in international cricket, winning the first T20I in Belfast by 34 runs. Lorcan Tucker (50) and Gareth Delany (49) guided Ireland to 182/9 before debutant Matt Hollard's 3/28 helped bowl out India for 148. Irfan Pathan expressed his surprise, while India captain Shreyas Iyer vowed to bounce back in the next match.

title: Can India break its habit of self-medication?
content: India grapples with widespread self-medication, a habit deeply ingrained in its healthcare culture. While responsible self-care for minor ailments is encouraged, the unsupervised use of prescription drugs, especially antibiotics, fuels antimicrobial resistance. Recent government regulations on cough syrups signal a push for safer medicine use, but experts emphasize the need for improved healthcare access and public awareness to break this dangerous dependency.

title: 'Let's die together': K'taka man pulls out bombs after lover 'refuses' to marry him, dies in blast
content: A tragic incident unfolded on a Karnataka highway as a 30-year-old man, Nagendra, died in a car explosion. Police suspect a relationship dispute led to Nagendra attacking his partner, Ramya, before detonating crude bombs. Ramya and the cab driver managed to escape the burning vehicle. Investigations are ongoing into the explosives and the events.

title: Michael Dell updates on Dell's Texas move days after Abbott's 'welcome home' message
content: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% of shareholders approving the change. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company's legal home to the state where it was founded. This move aligns with Texas's growing appeal for major corporations, including Tesla, Coinbase, and Exxon Mobil, due to its favorable business climate and talent pool.

title: Couple denied boarding despite valid passes, spent night at airport; airline to pay Rs 62,000
content: SpiceJet has been ordered by a Srinagar consumer commission to pay Rs 62,078 to a Haj couple denied boarding despite having valid passes. The airline, failing to appear, was found guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice. The couple, returning from pilgrimage, faced a night at the airport and rebooked flights at a higher cost due to the airline's inexplicable refusal to let them board.

title: 'No one drops catches on purpose': Shafali plays down costly dropped catches
content: Sunday's matches will decide which teams from Group A reach the semi-finals. South Africa, on six points, will face Bangladesh, who have four points. India, also on six points, will then take on unbeaten Australia, who have eight points.

title: When K Bhagyaraj argued with Amitabh Bachchan on Aakhree Raasta
content: The Indian film fraternity mourns the loss of celebrated writer-director K Bhagyaraj. His impactful Hindi directorial debut, 'Aakhree Raasta' (1986), starring Amitabh Bachchan, showcased his unique blend of family drama and emotion, even within a genre dominated by action. Bhagyaraj's meticulous approach and Bachchan's trust in his vision resulted in a critically acclaimed film, leaving a lasting legacy.

title: 'MCOCA against culprits': Shinde as 3 arrested in Maharashtra TET paper leak
content: Maharashtra's Teachers Eligibility Test (TET) was postponed just 24 hours before its scheduled date due to a suspected paper leak. Deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde vowed strict action, considering the stringent MCOCA.  Opposition leaders Rahul Gandhi and Arvind Kejriwal criticised the ruling BJP, highlighting a pattern of exam irregularities and questioning the government's ability to manage national systems.

title: Under-construction guest house slab collapses in UP; 1 killed, several feared trapped
content: A devastating slab collapse at an under-construction guest house in Barabanki's Haidergarh area has left at least eight workers injured and several others feared trapped. A massive rescue operation is underway, involving police, fire services, and the SDRF. Preliminary reports suggest a shuttering failure led to the incident, burying labourers under debris. Authorities are working tirelessly to locate and rescue those still missing.

title: 'I am a farmer': Union minister on Rs 99L subsidy row; Gehlot says 'new model of corruption'
content: Union Minister of State for Agriculture Bhagirath Choudhary has denied allegations of receiving a nearly Rs 1 crore subsidy from his ministry for his own farm, stating he applied years before becoming a minister and that the project is transparent. The claims have drawn sharp criticism from opposition leaders, who label it a "new model of corruption" and a conflict of interest within the government.

title: 'Not targeted at India': US envoy Sergio Gor defends Trump's H-1B visa changes
content: US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor assured that H-1B visa changes are not India-specific but part of a broader immigration review. He drew parallels with India's stance on illegal migration, emphasizing the US aims to secure its borders. Gor highlighted continued strong people-to-people ties, trade, and defense cooperation, despite the visa program's ongoing adjustments. The US embassy remains a busy hub for visa processing, underscoring the enduring bilateral relationship.

title: Backing without clarity: Ashwin questions Gambhir's 'wrong utilisation' of Sundar
content: In the first T20I against Ireland, he bowled only one over, introduced in the 16th over of the innings, and later scored 9 off 12 balls after coming in at No. 6.

title: 'Any exam left?' Opposition tears into BJP over Maharashtra TET 'paper leak'
content: Maharashtra's Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) exam, scheduled for June 28, has been postponed following a suspected paper leak in Bhiwandi. Police seized materials matching exam questions, prompting the Maharashtra State Council of Examination to cancel the test for transparency. Both the Cockroach Janta Party and Congress have slammed the BJP-led state government over the incident, calling it "incapable" and the "Paper Leak Government."

title: India’s family firms are booming — but who will lead them tomorrow?
content: Indian family businesses are experiencing unprecedented growth, expanding globally and embracing new technologies. However, a significant challenge looms: the next generation is increasingly opting for careers outside the family firm. While founders remain optimistic, only a small fraction of heirs wish to take the reins, signaling a critical need for evolving succession strategies to ensure these economic powerhouses continue to thrive.

title: Eye on Pakistan & China: Anti-submarine Eurodrone spurs India's layered maritime defence push
content: The development of a new anti-submarine warfare drone, a joint venture between Japan and Europe, highlights the growing importance of unmanned platforms in maritime security. This development is particularly relevant for India, facing expanding submarine fleets from Pakistan and China. The article emphasizes persistent surveillance, manned-unmanned teaming with existing assets like P-8I aircraft, and cost-effective coverage as key takeaways for strengthening India's naval defence capabilities.

title: GCL Season 4 Draft: Carlsen returns, Divya set for debut - all you need to know
content: The Global Chess League (GCL) Season 4 Player Draft will be held in Mumbai on June 29, with the tournament set to take place in Bengaluru from September 3-13. World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen returns after missing last season, while FIDE Women's World Cup champion Divya Deshmukh will make her debut. The player pool features 36 Grandmasters, including Viswanathan Anand and Ian Nepomniachtchi.

title: Has Hardik Pandya secretly married Mahieka Sharma? Viral photo fuels wedding rumours
content: Speculation is rife that cricketer Hardik Pandya has secretly married his girlfriend, Mahieka Sharma. Recent social media photos showing the couple with an elderly woman, where Mahieka appears to be wearing sindoor, have fueled these rumors. This comes after Pandya publicly confirmed his relationship with the model in October 2025, nearly two years after his separation from Natasa Stankovic.

title: Watch: Uruguay coach loses cool after FIFA World Cup 2026 exit
content: Marcelo Bielsa's future as Uruguay coach is under pressure after the team's early exit from the FIFA World Cup 2026. Uruguay failed to win a match in Group H, drawing against Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde before losing 1-0 to Spain. Bielsa also drew attention after reacting angrily to a journalist, before later accepting responsibility for the disappointing campaign.

title: Khalistani terror threat alert issued for Delhi, Uttarakhand: Report
content: Security agencies have issued a high alert for Delhi and Uttarakhand following intelligence indicating potential attacks by Khalistani terrorists. Temples, government buildings, railway stations, and police installations are identified as possible targets. The threat, reportedly received via email, has prompted heightened vigilance and security reviews across sensitive locations. Investigations are underway to verify the authenticity and origin of the threat.

title: 'Resignation received from Champat Rai': Ram Mandir Trust says 'shocked, hurt' by donation row
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust has received resignations from General Secretary Champat Rai and trustee Anil Mishra amidst allegations of donation embezzlement at the Ayodhya Ram Mandir. The trust assures devotees their offerings are safe and accounted for, while confirming an FIR has been registered following an SIT probe. Legal proceedings are underway to address the controversy and restore public confidence.

title: MQ‑28 Ghost Bat to take part in Pacific’s biggest wargame, faces first high-stakes test
content: India's future aerial combat capabilities are set to be revolutionized as the advanced MQ-28 Ghost Bat, a stealth drone, prepares for its first major international exercise, Valiant Shield 2026. This unmanned aircraft will fly alongside crewed fighters, enhancing their reach and survivability. Its integration promises to unlock new frontiers in manned-unmanned teaming, offering crucial insights for future warfare.

title: NFL ESPN's Matt Miller faces scam allegations after life-threatening accident
content: ESPN analyst Matt Miller has found himself in a troubling situation after he crashed his car into a semi truck in Missouri last week as he sustained serious injuries. The accident has been reported to be life threatening as he also ended up losing his left arm. While his family has opened a GoFundMe page to raise funds for his treatments, it seems things have taken a dark turn.

title: 'He asks questions all the time': Abhishek reveals what Sooryavanshi is like
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is making waves in Indian cricket after a stellar IPL 2026 and a record-breaking List A innings.  Despite a maiden T20I call-up for the Ireland tour, he was benched for the first match.  Teammate Abhishek Sharma praised his eagerness to learn, while the team's batting struggles in the opener have intensified calls for Sooryavanshi's debut.

title: 'Sooryavanshi is breathing down his neck': Samson gets blunt warning
content: Following India's T20I loss to Ireland, former player Sanjay Manjrekar warns Sanju Samson about the rising competition.  With young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the horizon, Samson faces pressure to perform.  While Sooryavanshi's talent is acknowledged, Manjrekar suggests he might not have single-handedly altered the game's outcome, emphasizing the difficulty of the chase and the current lack of openings at the top of the order.

title: 24 hours before exam, Maharashtra TET postponed after suspected paper leak in Thane
content: The Maharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) 2026, slated for June 28, has been postponed due to a suspected question paper leak. Bhiwandi police apprehended individuals with examination-related materials, which MSCE officials confirmed matched the TET paper. This breach of integrity prompted the council to reschedule the exam, with a new date to be announced soon. Candidates are urged to await official communication.

title: 'Do not need validation': PoK 'PM' mocks Pak defence minister over 'not proper Kashmiris' remark
content: The "Prime Minister" of Pakistan-occupied Kashmir, Faizal Mumtaz Rathore, has strongly criticized Pakistan's Defence Minister Khwaja Asif for questioning the Kashmiri identity of residents in Rawalkot and Mirpur. Rathore accused Asif of creating divisions and urged him to apologize for his remarks, rather than blaming local governance. This dispute unfolds amidst widespread protests in PoK over inflation and upcoming elections, where a joint committee has demanded significant reforms.

title: Romelu Lukaku becomes Belgium's highest FIFA WC scorer with record-breaking goal
content: Lukaku reached the milestone during Belgium's comfortable win, which helped them finish at the top of Group G.

title: Out on parole, POCSO convict arrested for raping 5-year-old in Uttarakhand
content: A man recently released on parole after a POCSO conviction has been apprehended for allegedly raping a five-year-old girl in Pithoragarh. The accused, Hosiyar Singh, a driver, was serving a seven-year sentence for a previous child sexual assault case. Police swiftly rescued the distressed child and arrested Singh within eight hours of the crime being reported. The victim is receiving care and treatment.

title: 13-year-old Ethiopian girl dies mid-air while travelling to Hyderabad for cancer treatment
content: Tragedy struck on an Ethiopian Airlines flight bound for Hyderabad as a 13-year-old girl, en route for cancer treatment, passed away mid-air. The young patient, who had battled cancer for three years, was pronounced dead by medical professionals upon the aircraft's arrival at Rajiv Gandhi International Airport early Saturday morning. Authorities confirmed the unfortunate incident.

title: Vaiko's MDMK exits DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance after nine years
content: The Marumalarchi Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (MDMK) has exited the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance after a nine-year partnership. The party cited ideological differences and a perceived weakening of its identity within the alliance as reasons for its departure. MDMK leaders expressed dissatisfaction with their treatment, including contesting elections under the DMK's symbol. The move follows a period of significant political shifts in Tamil Nadu after the recent Assembly elections.

title: Elephant released into forest found dead
content: The elephant, which was radio-collared and released into the forest after straying into human habitation, was found lying motionless on Saturday

title: Jagan accuses TDP government of orchestrating attack on YSRCP leaders
content: YSRCP chief says party leaders had visited Amaravati at farmers’ invitation; alleges police coordinated the attack and farmers are being harassed in the name of the capital project

title: Versatile actor, director, screenwriter K. Bhagyaraj no more
content: Mentored by the late Bharathiraja, Bhagyaraj changed the perception of the Tamil film hero, proving that wit, intelligence, and ordinariness could be as compelling as physical prowess and a striking presence

title: Sujata Rout no ordinary cadre, key to BJD’s revival push
content: If the BJD can rebuild its activities around a committed core group, it can regain its footing in Odisha, feel political commentators; with her personal contacts with leaders of women SHGs, Sujata Rout can regroup women for the party’s cause ahead of panchayat elections early next year

title: Four killed, eight injured in lightning strikes in Chhindwara, Madhya Pradesh
content: Police said Satish Walke (19) and Lucky Meravi (18) died on the spot in Bhamori near Birwa after being struck by a bolt from the sky, while Jhaam Singh Taram (34) succumbed to injuries during treatment in the hospital

title: Indian firm, CEO among entities sanctioned by U.S. for ‘fuelling’ civil war in Sudan
content: The U.S. Treasury Department’s Office of Foreign Assets Control said the targeted networks enabled both the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces to expand and intensify civil war

title: Uddhav calls for ‘BJP-mukt Ram’, breathes fire against the BJP over the Ram temple donations row
content: The Shiv Sena (UBT) chief slams BJP over its stand that a passport is not proof of citizenship, adding that voters need to question the defected MPs as well as the BJP, which wanted to finish off the Opposition

title: Akhilesh Yadav vows to restore Ayodhya’s eternal glory amid row
content: The Samajwadi Party says that ideas of Lord Ram are inherently socialist and these ideals have been followed by the party and its idealogical forefathers

title: Forest squads battle rise in illegal adventure trips in protected forests in Malabar region
content: The issue comes into focus again after three youths, who entered the Valook forest near Vilangad in Kozhikode district without authorisation on June 26, had to be rescued following overnight search operations

title: CBSE schools in Karnataka seek clarification on the implementation of three-language policy
content: On June 26, Union Minister for Education Dharmendra Pradhan announced the relaxation of three language policy for class 7, 8 and 9 CBSE students, and it will be applicable for class 6 students only

title: In a U-turn, Punjab government notifies VB-G RAM G scheme
content: In December 2025, the Bhagwant Mann government had convened a special session of the Punjab Assembly, which had unanimously passed a resolution against the VB-G RAM G Act

title: Telangana gears up for Pulse Polio drive targeting 40.97 lakh children on June 28
content: Health Minister Damodar Raja Narasimha says the drive should cover every child through fixed booths, mobile teams and door-to-door survey

title: Plagiarism row: K.R. Meera sends legal notice to Haritha Savithri
content: The row stems from allegations of similarities between the plots of K.R. Meera’s Kalachi and Haritha Savithri’s Zin

title: Two BJP-led States question wage burden under VB-G RAM G
content: Bihar, MP and Jharkhand question 60:40 funding pattern; five seek wage revision, four raise concerns over non-working days; most flag pending dues

title: PM Modi hands over 'Made in India' patrol vessel to Seychelles Coast Guard
content: The Ministry of External Affairs said that PM Modi also handed over six ambulances, 10 utility vehicles and five laser radial boats, which it said would further contribute to Seychelles’ development and security

title: Unqualified practitioners found administering steroids, painkillers at three clinics in Hyderabad
content: The Council appealed to citizens to report suspected quacks through its WhatsApp helpline at 9154382727

title: Plea in Supreme Court seeks creation of fire safety framework for high-risk public buildings
content: Petitioner says right to life under Article 21 is not a right to be rescued after a fire, but a right not to be knowingly exposed to preventable fires by a State with requisite power, codes, duty to prevent such danger

title: Naidu disburses ₹300 crore R&R funds to Veligonda project-displaced families
content: Thanks to the sacrifices made by the affected people, the project helps in providing irrigation water to 4.47 lakh acres and drinking water to 23 lakh people, and  transform the fortunes of four districts, says the Chief Minister

title: NHRC seeks reports from DGP, Chief Secretary on Sai Krishna custodial death
content: Rights panel flags possible violation of Article 21; seeks details of SIT probe, action against accused police personnel, while teams fan out to arrest head constables allegedly involved in destroying evidence

title: Kharge accuses Centre of ‘looting’ the public as fuel prices stay high despite fall in global crude oil rate
content: It is evident that the public has become merely a means for the BJP government to extract taxes and increase its collections, says Kharge

title: Maharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test postponed day before exam after suspicion of paper leak
content: The decision to postpone the exam a day before the exam was taken after an investigation and search by the Bhiwandi Police in Thane district found people in possession of questions similar to those in the TET paper

title: Be wary of divisive forces bent on using caste for political gain, warns Pawan Kalyan
content: The YSR Congress Party will do all sorts of political manipulation in the name of caste to undermine the coalition government, and the JSP cadres need to thwart such designs, says the Deputy Chief Minister

title: Congress accuses Rajnath Singh of misleading Parliament on Operation Sindoor casualties
content: The Centre’s disclosure exposes a contradiction between the Defence Minister’s statement in Parliament and the official record, the Opposition party says, calling for a full disclosure of facts in the monsoon session

title: Delhi among five States starting SIR enumeration on June 30
content: The other States beginning the SIR process are Karnataka, Meghalaya, Maharashtra, and Jharkhand; the five States have a cumulative voter base of 19.79 crore

title: Prakasam Collector assures tobacco procurement, warns companies against ‘no bids’
content: Raja Babu tells farmers every tobacco bale will be purchased, says only companies procuring tobacco this season will be allowed to participate in next year’s auctions

title: Cancel clearance given to NMDC-KIOM, urges SPS, JMM
content: ‘Strict action should be taken against mining companies in Chitradurga district as they were involved in serious violations’

title: Karnataka opens farmer registration for Totapuri mangoes under MIP scheme
content: This scheme will be in force for a period of one month only from the date of the first transaction of mango produce in the software

title: Dravidian University unveils ₹364 crore plan for academic transformation
content: Addressing a press conference, Vice-Chancellor says roadmap has been submitted to the State government for financial concurrence

title: YSRCP leaders’ visit to Amaravati villages blocked by farmers, scuffle breaks out
content: The leaders had gone under the CRDA Rythu Parirakshana Committee banner; farmers said they had no right to visit, and a constable was reportedly injured in stone-throwing

title: Govt. to launch affordable digital health management system for small clinics across India
content: eSushrut@Clinic is a light Hospital Management Information System (HMIS) which will provide a simple digital solution specifically tailored for small outpatient clinics; over 800 health facilities are currently boarded on eSushrut@Clinic, generating over 680 health records

title: RTIH proving to be a game-changer for startups, says NTR District Collector
content: Lakshmisha says the State is promoting entrepreneurship by connecting innovators with industry and helping startups access mentorship, technical support and markets

title: Air India May Restore Some International Flights As Tensions In Gulf Ease
content: Air India reduced services on select international routes between June and August this year amid airspace restrictions over parts of the Middle East and record-high international jet fuel prices.

title: 10,000 Vespas Swarm Rome's Historic Sites To Mark Scooter's 80th Anniversary
content: Vespa-borne visitors converged on the Eternal City's cobblestone streets to celebrate a brand they likewise view as timeless. If for only a day, Ferrari and Ducati were forgotten as the little Vespa left them in its dust.

title: Delhi-NCR Earthquake LIVE Updates: 6.2 Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Afghanistan, Tremors Felt In Delhi
content: 6.2 Magnitude Earthquake hit Delhi Live Updates: According to the National Centre for Seismology, a 6.2 magnitude earthquake struck Afghanistan at 7:04 IST on Saturday.

title: Behind Siya Goyal-Ketan Agarwal Matchmaking, Role Of "Mittal Family" In Focus
content: The police said Siya was in a relationship with Chetan and considered Agarwal an impediment. Siya allegedly signalled Chetan at the fort, who pushed him off a cliff.

title: MacBook, iPad Price Hikes Only The Beginning: Here's What You May Pay Next
content: An unprecedented AI data centre boom is triggering a massive $670 billion surge in the global memory market, expanding it to $890 billion by 2026, according to Morgan Stanley.

title: Collapsed Buildings, Debris: Satellite Pics Show Impact Of Venezuela Earthquakes
content: High-resolution satellite imagery sourced from Vantor confirms the widespread structural damage caused by two deadly earthquakes in Venezuela.

title: Maharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test Postponed After Paper Leak, 3 Arrested
content: The Teacher Eligibility Test 2026 in Maharashtra was postponed on Saturday, just a day before it was scheduled to take place, after parts of the question paper were allegedly leaked.

title: Couple, Toddler Found Dead In Kerala River, Search On For Missing Daughter
content: The police on Saturday recovered the body of a man from the Muvattupuzha river, a day after the bodies of his visually impaired wife and their two-year-old son were found floating in the water at Piravom near here, officials said.

title: "Playhouse For Predator": Epstein Used 'Fake' Science Office As Prison Escape
content: The Florida Science Foundation was presented as a legitimate organisation focused on supporting research. But a review by The Telegraph of more than 1,000 documents connected to the company painted a different picture.

title: "They Humiliated Us": MDMK Chief On Quitting DMK Alliance In Tamil Nadu
content: The MDMK and the DMK started working together in December 2017. They had called the partnership a defence against communal forces and a safeguard for the Dravidian movement's ideological core

title: Man, 22, Arrested For Raping, Threatening 14-Year-Old Girl In UP: Cops
content: A man has been arrested here on Saturday for allegedly raping a 14-year-old girl in a village here, police said.

title: Small Plane Crash At Beijing's Tallest Building Kills Pilot, Injures 13
content: It was unclear whether the injured were in the building or were hit by debris, but the statement said they were receiving treatment.

title: Punjab BJP Chief Praises Maharashtra's Move To Consult Sikhs On Takht Law
content: His remarks came as Punjab leaders, including former Union minister Harsimrat Kaur Badal, Congress MP Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa and Akali Dal Waris Punjab De leader Manpreet Singh Ayali, opposed the proposed law.

title: Karnataka Man Stabs Woman In Car, He Dies After Vehicle Explodes
content: According to preliminary information, the man booked the car in Bengaluru and then travelled with a woman

title: "Blatant Violation Of Peace Deal": Iran Slams US After Strikes
content: Iran has warned vessels not to enter or leave the Gulf through the strait without permission, but ships have continued to move, some using a route not authorised by Tehran.

title: Oil Tanker Hit By "Unknown Projectile" In Hormuz As Iran, US Exchange Fire
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guards announced on Saturday that they had attacked American positions in the Gulf after US strikes on Iran.

title: YSRCP Alleges Attack By TDP Supporters Over Amaravati Land Row
content: The incident triggered a political blame game between the ruling TDP and the opposition YSRCP over the ongoing land acquisition dispute in the capital region

content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: India’s family structures have changed but this Census question has not
content: who is the head of your family? the question means different things to different people . for some families, the answer comes instinctively: the father or the grandfather . in many homes, everyone looks at each other before naming someone .

title: 'Operation Devendra': Uddhav says Shah planned Sena (UBT) MP defections to 'sideline' Fadnavis
content: uddhav Thackeray accused the BJP's so-called "Operation Tiger" the defection of six Lok Sabha MPs from his faction was aimed at keeping Fadnavis "in check" if the need arises, these rebels will vote for amnesty international tomorrow .
